author: Synaphi
description: Keep Obsidian running in the system tray instead of quitting when the window is closed. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Synaphi
manifest.description: Keep running in the system tray instead of quitting when you close the window.
manifest.name: Background Tray

name: Background Tray

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.6

- **Fix (important):** relaunching Obsidian from the taskbar while it was hidden in the tray could **quit the running instance** (the tray icon vanished and a second click was needed). The vault-switcher window Obsidian creates during relaunch is now hidden before it paints and closed only after the existing window is safely restored, so the app can no longer auto-quit. Root cause: an Electron `window-all-closed` race from closing the switcher window too early (regression introduced in 1.0.5).
- **Strictly single-purpose:** removed the command-palette entries, so Obsidian no longer shows the per-plugin **Hotkeys** button. Everything stays on the tray icon's right-click menu (Show/Hide, Relaunch, Quit completely).

Assets (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) are attached individually with build-provenance attestation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## What's new in 1.0.5

- **Smoother relaunch.** Reopening Obsidian from the taskbar while it's hidden in the tray no longer flickers — the vault switcher window is suppressed before it ever appears.
- **Cleaner tray tooltip.** Default is now `{{vault}} - Background Tray` (was `... | Obsidian`).
- **Lighter docs & new banner.** README now emphasizes the plugin's single-purpose, lightweight design; higher-resolution banner.
- **Note:** keeping Obsidian running in the tray lets Obsidian Sync keep working in the background on its own.

Assets (`main.js`, `manifest.json`, `styles.css`) are attached individually with build-provenance attestation.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Background Tray v1.0.4**

Code-quality pass to clear the directory review's source-code warnings (no behaviour change):
- Type-safe Electron access (narrow interfaces instead of `any`; no unsafe member/call/assignment warnings).
- Access Electron via `window.require` (avoids forbidden `require()` imports); use `window.setTimeout`.
- Dropped the `builtin-modules` dependency in favour of Node's built-in `node:module`.

Same features as v1.0.1+ (run in background, tray menu, single-instance focus, PayPal funding). Build provenance attestations included.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
**Background Tray v1.0.1** — first public release.

Keep Obsidian running in the system tray instead of quitting when you close the window.

### Features
- **Run in background** — closing the window (X) hides Obsidian to the tray instead of quitting.
- **Tray icon** — left-click toggles show/hide; right-click menu: Show / Hide, Relaunch, Quit completely. Uses Obsidian's own app icon.
- **Single-instance focus** — relaunching Obsidian while it's hidden restores the existing window instead of opening the vault switcher.
- **Custom tray icon & tooltip** — `{{vault}}` is replaced with the vault name.
- Bindable commands: Toggle window, Quit completely, Relaunch Obsidian.
- Turning the plugin off restores all default behaviour (no leftover listeners).

> Desktop only. Windows and macOS fully supported; Linux tray behaviour is best-effort.

### Install (manual)
Copy `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` into `<vault>/.obsidian/plugins/background-tray/`, then enable under Settings -> Community plugins.
